summary: Phase 1: Implemented all 7 console commands, created 22 backend unit test files with 103 test methods. Phase 2: Created browser-based frontend test suite (8 HTML test files, 78 test methods), 6 PHPUnit API integration test files (41 test methods), and updated all documentation.
requirements_addressed: Console command: order:expire -- auto-expire pending_match orders older than 30 minutes with audit logging
Console command: governance:dedup -- deduplicate behavior events within 1-minute windows with lineage
Console command: governance:fill -- fill missing listing counters from behavior events with lineage
Console command: governance:lineage -- record daily lineage summary (meta-lineage)
Console command: governance:quality -- compute 7 data quality metrics per organization
Console command: credibility:recompute -- recompute credibility scores for all reviews with lineage
Console command: search:build-dictionary -- rebuild search dictionary from active listing content
PHPUnit configuration with unit and api test suites
Backend unit test suite: 22 files, 103 test methods covering Tiers 1-3
Frontend test suite: 8 browser-based HTML test files with 78 test methods
Frontend test: role-based navigation (8 tests) -- verifies nav items per role, login redirect, logout
Frontend test: listing form validation (13 tests) -- title/address/rider/vehicle/time/tags/draft vs publish
Frontend test: listing workflow (6 tests) -- draft/publish/unpublish API calls, change preview, version timeline, diff view
Frontend test: search/filter/sort (13 tests) -- suggestions, highlights, did-you-mean, no-results fallback, filters, sort, search history
Frontend test: order lifecycle (13 tests) -- action buttons per status, blocked messages, 5-min cancel, 72h dispute, admin-only resolve
Frontend test: review form (14 tests) -- rating 1-5, text limits, char counter, file count/size/type, submit state, error messages
Frontend test: moderation queue (9 tests) -- access control, flag colors, credibility score colors, approve/reject/escalate, empty state
Frontend test: governance dashboard (7 tests) -- admin-only access, metric cards, empty state, lineage rows, PII toggle and masking
API integration test: AuthApiTest (8 tests) -- register, login, logout, /me, duplicate email, invalid org code
API integration test: ListingApiTest (13 tests) -- CRUD, publish/unpublish, bulk close, search/filter/sort
API integration test: OrderApiTest (11 tests) -- full lifecycle, cancel rules (409+40901), dispute window, admin-only resolve
API integration test: ReviewApiTest (6 tests) -- create, rate limit 429, duplicate detection, file count/size
API integration test: MediaApiTest (4 tests) -- signed URL valid/expired/tampered, hotlink protection
API integration test: PermissionApiTest (6 tests) -- RBAC enforcement for moderation, governance, audit, users endpoints
Documentation: test-coverage.md updated with frontend and API test inventories
Documentation: screen-spec.md updated with implementation note
Documentation: route-inventory.md updated with frontend implementation mapping
Documentation: reviewer-notes.md updated with Section 6 Frontend Review Guide
Documentation: README.md updated with frontend architecture, running instructions, and test instructions

decisions.what: Browser-based test files with self-contained test harness (no npm/node required)
decisions.why: Offline environment without test runners; HTML files can be opened directly in any browser for immediate visual feedback

decisions.what: Each frontend test file includes mock versions of RCAuth, RCApi, RCUtil, RCRouter
decisions.why: Tests must be self-contained and not depend on the full frontend application loading; mocks allow testing UI logic in isolation

decisions.what: API integration tests use curl for HTTP calls rather than Guzzle or other libraries
decisions.why: Minimize external dependencies; curl is available in all PHP environments

decisions.what: OrderApiTest.test_cancel_blocked_in_progress asserts both 409 status and 40901 error code
decisions.why: This is the single most critical business rule; the test must verify both the HTTP status and the application error code

decisions.what: Frontend test-harness.js uses IIFE pattern with render() method
decisions.why: Compatible with older browsers without module support; matches the Layui frontend's coding style

open_risks: Service classes (AuthService, OrderService, ListingService, etc.) not yet implemented -- tests verify logic patterns that services must follow
Model classes not yet implemented -- commands reference models that need to be created
Controller classes not yet implemented -- routes point to controllers that need to be scaffolded
Composer dependencies not installed (vendor/ directory) -- phpunit binary not yet available
Database migrations not yet created -- schema for all 18+ models pending
Frontend page modules (18 of 22) not yet implemented -- only listings.js and orders.js exist
Frontend shared modules (api.js, auth.js, util.js, router.js) not yet implemented
Docker Compose environment not yet tested end-to-end
API integration tests require seeded test database with known user accounts and completed orders
notes: Phase 2 complete: frontend test suite and API integration tests written. All 8 frontend test HTML files contain real assertions against simulated UI logic matching the screen-spec. All 6 API test files contain real PHPUnit assertions against documented API endpoints. Documentation fully updated across 5 files. Grand total across all test suites: 22 backend unit tests (103 methods) + 8 frontend tests (78 methods) + 6 API tests (41 methods) = 36 test files, 222 test methods.
